event_blocks.summary: The narrative begins with aerial views of New York City's skyline, transitioning to a man, John, expressing his determination to provide a safe home for his baby and a better life than he had. A man holds a baby in a pink jacket at a playground, juxtaposed with a crowded subway car where a British-accented narrator notes the city's growing unhoused population. An older man in a suit comments on the ease of becoming homeless, and the narrator states that over 650,000 people are homeless in the US, with the situation being particularly severe in New York. A nighttime view of Times Square precedes a title card: 'My Way: Young and Homeless in New York,' by Benjamin Alvarez Gruber. The perspective shifts to a first-person view as John, wearing a Led Zeppelin t-shirt, enters a small, cluttered apartment, describing it as his living space. Inside, the cramped room contains a bed, kitchenette, and baby items. John's girlfriend, Zoe, in a maroon top, tenderly holds their baby daughter, wrapped in a white blanket. The narrator reveals John lives with his family in a Brooklyn homeless shelter, sharing the room. Later, inside the small apartment, Zoe gently interacts with the baby. John describes the cramped space, showing his kitchenette, the provided fridge, a small table, a dresser, and a bed, before preparing a meal. The narrator states they have lived there for a year and are working to move into their own place, with John explaining that delays in public assistance are hindering their efforts to secure an apartment. The scene shifts outdoors to a sunny, grassy area where John, in a dark puffer jacket, walks with Zoe, in a white coat and hood, and their baby, in a pink jacket with floral embroidery. The narrator reveals John was kicked out of his family home at 21 and stayed with friends.

Baby in apartment.description: A baby, possibly named Isabella, wrapped in a white blanket, being held and gently interacted with by the woman in the maroon top, lying on her lap in a cramped living space, later seen in a pink jacket with floral embroidery outdoors.

Woman with baby in apartment.description: The same woman, now wearing a white coat and hood, walking outdoors with the man and baby in a sunny, grassy area near a fence.

Man in suit.description: An older man with dark hair and a mustache, wearing a black suit and white shirt, speaking indoors in a brightly lit, possibly commercial or office setting, stating in a distinct male voice, 'But it's real easy to be homeless these days.'

Man with baby at playground.description: A young man with dark curly hair, wearing a dark puffer jacket, holding a baby in a pink jacket with floral embroidery, standing in a sunny outdoor playground.

Apartment interior.description: A small, cluttered studio apartment, described by its resident John as containing his kitchen, a provided fridge, a small table, a dresser, and a bed, reflecting a lived-in, tight space where the family has resided for a year.

Man with Led Zeppelin shirt.description: A young man, identified as John, with short dark hair, wearing a black t-shirt with 'Led Zeppelin' printed in white, who films himself in a hallway and enters a small apartment, describing it as 'my kitchen,' 'the fridge,' 'a little table,' 'a dresser,' and 'a bed.' He is later seen cooking in the kitchen and discussing his family's struggle to get public assistance for a new apartment, before appearing outdoors in a dark puffer jacket holding the baby.

Times Square at night.description: A vibrant, crowded urban scene with towering digital billboards advertising Disney+, H&M, and musicals, under a dark sky, filled with pedestrians and city lights.

raw_segments: The video opens with sweeping aerial shots of New York City’s iconic skyline, showcasing skyscrapers like the Empire State Building and Chrysler Building under a clear blue sky, accompanied by ethereal, slightly melancholic background music. A young man, later identified as John, is heard speaking with a determined tone, stating his commitment to providing a "safe roof over her head" for his baby, finding an apartment, and ensuring she gets "the life that I never had." Visually, the scene transitions to a man holding a baby in a pink jacket at a playground, suggesting this personal, intimate moment amidst the city. A brief glimpse inside a crowded subway car highlights the city’s public transit life, as a female narrator with a British accent explains that "New York, the city of big dreams for many is also home to a growing number of unhoused people." The video then cuts to an older man in a suit speaking indoors, who adds, "But it's real easy to be homeless these days." The narrator continues, revealing that "Over 650,000 people are homeless in the US, more than ever before," and that "The situation is especially bad in New York." The visuals shift to a nighttime view of Times Square, bustling with bright billboards and crowds, while the background music becomes more prominent and somber. A title card appears: 'My Way: Young and Homeless in New York,' crediting 'Benjamin Alvarez Gruber.' The narrative then shifts to a first-person, handheld camera perspective, showing John, the man in the black Led Zeppelin t-shirt, walking down a hallway and entering a small, cluttered apartment. John's voice is heard again, stating, "And so this is where I live." Inside, the space is cramped with a bed, kitchenette, and baby items. A woman, identified by the narrator as John's girlfriend Zoe, in a maroon top is seen sitting in a chair, tenderly holding their baby daughter wrapped in a white blanket, emphasizing the intimate, domestic reality of young parenthood in a tight living space. The narrator confirms that "John lives with his family in a homeless shelter in New York," sharing "this room in Brooklyn with his girlfriend Zoe and their daughter," underscoring themes of homelessness and resilience within their constrained environment.

The video opens with a tender moment inside a small, cluttered apartment where a woman in a maroon top gently interacts with a baby, possibly named Isabella, wrapped in a white blanket. A male voice, later identified as John, then begins to describe the cramped living space, filming himself in a hallway before entering and gesturing towards "my kitchen," "the fridge that they provide in the unit," "a little table," "a dresser," and "a bed." He moves through the apartment, showing the tight quarters before the camera focuses on him preparing a meal in the kitchen, adding tomato sauce to a pan with chicken. A female narrator explains that "it's been a year since they moved in here" and they are "working hard to be able to move into their own place as soon as possible." John further elaborates on their struggle, stating, "right now we're trying to get help getting an apartment, but it's taken a little while now because the public assistance is backed up with a bunch of cases. So, right now we're just waiting to get help that we need." The scene transitions to an outdoor setting, shifting from the intimate indoor space to a bright, open environment, as John, now in a dark puffer jacket, holds the baby (in a pink jacket with floral embroidery) and walks alongside the woman (in a white coat and hood) in a sunny, grassy area near a fence. The narrator then reveals that "John was kicked out of his family home when he was 21" and "he stayed with friends for a..."

event_blocks.summary: A woman tenderly cradles a baby in a white blanket while a narrator discusses homelessness, transitioning to a young man with dark curly hair in a dark puffer jacket and black hoodie. Standing outdoors in a sunlit urban area with a concrete wall and sparse vegetation, amidst city traffic sounds, he animatedly explains to the camera the arduous process of finding shelter, detailing three attempts and citing issues at previous locations such as run-down areas, lack of security, and cramped, shoulder-to-shoulder units, which were particularly difficult as his companion was pregnant at the time. He notes a later shelter was more convenient with more space. The scene then shifts to a bustling, brightly lit Times Square at night, with prominent advertisements for Disney+, H&M, and musicals, and an 'I ❤️ NY' gift shop. Amidst passing pedestrians, a person is seen sleeping on the sidewalk under a red and blue sleeping bag, a stark contrast to the commercial vibrancy. A somber instrumental track begins as the narrator returns, revealing that homelessness in New York is at its highest in nearly a century, with over 90,000 unhoused individuals currently in city shelters.

Baby in apartment.description: The baby, wrapped in a white blanket, is being held and gently interacted with by the woman, lying on her lap in a close, intimate setting, its presence implicitly linked to the man's mention of his pregnant partner.

Woman with baby in apartment.description: The same woman, now seen in a close-up, gently holding a baby wrapped in a white blanket, her face expressing tenderness and care, with soft lighting highlighting her features, her presence implicitly linked to the man's testimony about his pregnant partner and the broader narrative of homelessness.

Man with dark curly hair in puffer jacke.description: A young man with dark curly hair, wearing a dark puffer jacket over a black hoodie, standing outdoors in a sunlit urban setting. He speaks directly to the camera with an earnest and animated expression, gesturing, describing the poor conditions and lack of space in homeless shelters, and mentioning his partner was pregnant, all while city traffic sounds are audible in the background.

Times Square at night.description: A vibrant, crowded urban scene with towering digital billboards advertising Disney+, H&M, and musicals, under a dark sky, filled with pedestrians and city lights, including the 'I ❤️ NY' gift shop and a person sleeping on the sidewalk under a red and blue sleeping bag. This scene is accompanied by a somber instrumental track and a female narrator providing statistics on homelessness in New York.

raw_segments: The video opens with a close-up of a woman gently cradling a baby wrapped in a white blanket, her face showing tenderness as a female narrator explains the challenges of homelessness, mentioning someone landing in a shelter after being unable to afford rent. The scene transitions to a young man with dark curly hair, wearing a dark puffer jacket over a black hoodie, standing outdoors in a sunlit urban environment with a concrete wall and sparse vegetation. Amidst the sounds of city traffic, he speaks directly to the camera with an earnest and animated expression, gesturing as he recounts the difficulties of finding suitable shelter, stating, "We had to do like a few hurdles... it took us like three tries to get to this shelter." He describes previous shelters as "not suitable" due to "run down areas," a "lack of security," and "really, really small" "shoulder to shoulder" units, especially challenging because "at the time she was pregnant." He notes a later shelter was "more convenient" with "a little bit more space." The setting then shifts dramatically to a vibrant nighttime Times Square, where bright digital billboards advertise Disney+, H&M, and musicals, and the 'I ❤️ NY' gift shop is prominent. As pedestrians walk past, the camera pans to reveal a person sleeping on the sidewalk under a red and blue sleeping bag, powerfully juxtaposing commercial energy with urban hardship. A somber instrumental track begins as the female narrator returns, stating, "Homelessness in New York is at its highest level in almost 100 years. Over 90,000 unhoused New Yorkers are currently sleeping in the city's homeless shelters."

event_blocks.summary: In Times Square at night, amidst towering digital billboards and a melancholic soundtrack, a narrator highlights the escalating homelessness crisis, noting that a third of the homeless are children and the number of homeless adult New Yorkers has doubled, increasing demand for shelter among young families, as a person sleeps on the sidewalk. The scene shifts to a bright, sunny suburban street where John, a man with dark curly hair in a dark puffer jacket, carefully crosses the street holding a baby in a pink hooded jacket. He explains to the camera that his sisters used the shelter system due to housing instability, inspiring him to believe he could also manage. John, who works nights at a clinic and witnesses homelessness daily, shares that people seek hospital beds for shelter and food, and he sometimes has to ask them to leave, a difficult part of his job given his own past homelessness. He adjusts the baby's hood while the child looks around curiously. John then hands the baby to Zoe, a woman with a dark headscarf, who cradles the child tenderly in the sunlight. The narrator reveals John's past legal troubles and imprisonment, and Zoe's unwavering support since high school, as a school bus passes on the tree-lined street. Later, in a sunny suburban playground, John sits on a bench with the baby, while Zoe, wearing a white coat, smiles and converses with him. John reflects on how Zoe helped him discover an inner strength and 'fire' he didn't know he possessed, a sentiment Zoe gently affirms. He describes his inner strength as a growing 'furnace' and commits to being a 'rock' and 'sense of stability' for his family, stating, 'this is my family now,' as the baby sleeps peacefully in his arms.

Baby in apartment.description: The baby, in a pink hooded jacket with red trim, is being held by the man, appearing calm and possibly asleep, with its face serene and nestled against the man’s chest. The woman confirms 'She's asleep,' and the man refers to the baby as 'this one,' expressing his commitment to being a 'rock' and 'stability' for 'them' (his family).

Suburban street setting.description: A quiet, sunlit suburban street with brick houses, bare trees, a crosswalk, and a passing yellow school bus, creating a peaceful and family-friendly atmosphere that serves as the backdrop for John's personal reflections on homelessness and his family's struggles, delivered with an earnest voice, against the continued somber background music.

Woman with baby in apartment.description: The same woman, seen outdoors wearing a white coat with a dark hood, her long dark hair visible, sitting beside the man on the bench, smiling gently and engaging in conversation with him. She speaks in a gentle and supportive voice, offering encouraging remarks to the man and playfully confirming the baby's sleep.

Man with dark curly hair in puffer jacke.description: John, a young man with dark curly hair, wearing a dark puffer jacket over a black hoodie, holding a baby in a pink hooded jacket, walking across a suburban street and speaking earnestly to the camera about his family's experience with the shelter system and his work confronting homelessness, revealing his own past experience with homelessness.

Times Square at night.description: A vibrant, crowded urban scene with towering digital billboards advertising Disney+, H&M, and musicals, under a dark sky, filled with pedestrians and city lights, including the 'I ❤️ NY' gift shop and a person sleeping on the sidewalk under a red and blue sleeping bag, all set to somber, melancholic background music and narration detailing the rising numbers of homeless New Yorkers, including children and young families.

Man with baby at playground.description: A young man with dark curly hair, wearing a dark puffer jacket over a black hoodie, holding a baby in a pink hooded jacket with floral embroidery, seated on a wooden bench in a sunny suburban playground, engaging in conversation with a woman beside him. He speaks in a deep, calm, and reflective voice, sharing his personal growth, the woman's positive influence on him, and his commitment to being a 'rock,' protector, and provider for his family.

raw_segments: The segment opens with a stark visual contrast in Times Square at night, where towering digital billboards advertising brands like Disney+, H&M, and 'The Lion King' musical illuminate a bustling scene of pedestrians, while a somber, melancholic background music plays. A female narrator highlights the growing crisis of homelessness, stating that 'a third of them are children' and that 'the number of homeless adult New Yorkers has doubled,' increasing demand for shelter among young families, as a person is seen sleeping on the sidewalk under a red and blue sleeping bag. The scene then transitions to a bright, sunny day in a quiet suburban neighborhood, where John, a young man with dark curly hair, wearing a dark puffer jacket over a black hoodie, carefully crosses a street while holding a baby in a pink hooded jacket. He pauses at the pedestrian signal and speaks directly to the camera with an earnest voice, explaining, 'My sisters all went through the shelter system because they all have kids and you know they couldn't like stay where they were living... they couldn't live like with my mom because there's no space there. So they had to go through the shelter too and that's kind of what made me want to say, 'Hey, if they could do it, I could do it too.'' The narrator then reveals John 'works nights at a clinic where he is often confronted with homelessness,' as John continues, 'More people are going into the hospital just because they want a bed, they want something to eat... the hospital can't deny them, but it's unfortunate 'cause sometimes I have to like kick them out 'cause that's like a part of my job even though I don't want to 'cause I understand what it is to be homeless since I experienced that myself.' He occasionally adjusts the baby’s hood, and the baby looks around curiously. John then hands the baby to Zoe, a woman wearing a dark headscarf, who gently cradles the child and tenderly touches its face, the baby appearing calm and content in the soft sunlight. The narrator concludes by mentioning that 'In his teens, John broke the law. He was arrested and sent to prison. Zoe, whom he met in high school, never gave up on him,' as a school bus passes by on the peaceful, tree-lined street with brick houses.

The video opens with a serene outdoor scene in a sunny suburban playground, where a young man with dark curly hair, wearing a dark puffer jacket over a black hoodie, sits on a wooden bench holding a baby in a pink hooded jacket with floral embroidery. The baby, appearing calm and possibly asleep, is nestled securely in his arms. Beside him sits a woman wearing a white coat with a dark hood, her long dark hair visible, who smiles gently and engages in conversation with the man. A faint, high-pitched synth sound provides a gentle background as the man, speaking in a deep, calm, and reflective voice, shares how he never thought he was a good person until the woman helped him realize he's a strong guy who does everything right. He explains that she made him find a 'fire' within himself that he didn't know he had. The woman, with a gentle and supportive voice, interjects, 'You already had that fire,' to which the man humbly responds that he didn't realize it until she helped him acknowledge it, and since then, that inner fire has only grown stronger. As the camera slowly zooms in, capturing the intimate and familial atmosphere, the man then refers to the baby he holds, saying, 'And then with this one,' prompting the woman to playfully confirm, 'She's asleep.' He continues, describing his inner strength as a 'furnace' that will keep burning, emphasizing his unwavering commitment to be 'the rock' and a 'sense of stability' for them, no matter what challenges he faces. He concludes by stating, 'this is my family now,' expressing his desire to be their protector and provider, all while the baby remains still and serene in his arms, bathed in the soft sunlight.

event_blocks.summary: A young man with dark curly hair cradles a calm baby in a pink hooded jacket on a wooden bench in a sunny suburban playground, while a woman in a white coat sits beside him conversing, over which a male voice states, "And I'm gonna do everything in my power to do that," followed by a female voice sharing a personal testimony of belief in someone despite others' negative opinions. The scene transitions to an urban street where the man walks past the 'AVENUES FOR JUSTICE' building, identified by a narrator as a non-profit run by Andrés Rodriguez that keeps young people out of prison and provides alternatives to incarceration. Inside the warmly lit office, decorated with holiday garlands and a small Christmas tree, the man interacts with Andrés Rodriguez, an older man in a black suit and mustache, who earnestly discusses economic issues and homelessness, noting the difficulty of affording housing and the contrast with past neighborhood conditions. The Man in suit, seated in the office, speaks directly to the camera about rising apartment costs and the lack of housing, holding a small white object and gesturing expressively. A narrator details how the COVID-19 pandemic worsened conditions, leading to evictions and citing job termination, domestic violence, mental illness, and addiction as reasons for homelessness, mentioning "Ankhel" has helped young people for 50 years. The Man in suit explains his role as a court advocacy program that intersects with homelessness. The video then shows a close-up of the Man in suit discussing representing young people facing problems at home or self-created issues that prevent them from returning home. The camera reveals more of the office with youth program participants working on computers, one using a ThinkPad laptop, and a computer screen displaying a form asking about desired salary. A framed photograph of young people in a classroom is shown. A Woman in blue shirt, with long dark hair and a smartwatch, explains the center offers a safe space for homework, lunch, and CV improvement, with access to counselors as positive role models. She introduces Elsie, a former participant who began selling drugs at 13 and was arrested multiple times by 16. Elsie, in the office, reflects on finding out she was pregnant at 16, which changed her life and motivated her to get back into school and secure housing, preparing for young motherhood while facing state prison time. The scene shifts to Elsie at a long table in the communal office space, working on a laptop, with a man in a gray hoodie eating and drinking nearby. A narrator states Elsie is now helping others, dealing with a client's case in Connecticut. Elsie makes a phone call, asking about a court appearance and its outcome. The Woman in a blue shirt, tense and concerned, continues the phone call, questioning release conditions and the need for a heads-up if they need to travel. She concludes the call and turns to the camera, explaining the differences between New York and Connecticut systems and asserting that the job requires more than just showing up, involving a mental fight against an unfair system, particularly for minorities, concluding that home is not safe for most of these kids.

Suburban street setting.description: A quiet, sunlit suburban street with brick houses, bare trees, a crosswalk, and a passing yellow school bus, creating a peaceful and family-friendly atmosphere.

Baby in apartment.description: The baby, now in a pink hooded jacket with red trim, is being held by the man, appearing calm and possibly asleep, with its face serene and nestled against the man’s chest.

Man in suit.description: An older man with dark hair and a mustache, wearing a black suit and white shirt, seated in the Office interior, speaking expressively while holding a small white object and gesturing with his hands, discussing the issue of youth homelessness and the challenges young people face.

Man with baby at playground.description: A young man with dark curly hair, wearing a dark puffer jacket over a black hoodie, holding a baby in a pink hooded jacket with floral embroidery, seated on a wooden bench in a sunny suburban playground, engaging in conversation with a woman beside him.

Avenues for Justice building.description: A blue brick building with a white sign reading 'AVENUES FOR JUSTICE' and 'Andrew Cuomo Youth Program' beneath it, located on a city street. It is identified by a narrator as the non-profit organization run by Andrés Rodriguez, dedicated to keeping young people out of prison and providing alternatives to incarceration.

Office interior.description: A warmly lit office space with holiday decorations, including a golden wreath on the window, a printer on a cabinet, and a large 'Andrew Cuomo' banner on the wall, featuring a long table where the Woman in blue shirt works on a black ThinkPad laptop and speaks on the phone.

Green folder.description: A green folder containing printed materials and images, held and flipped through by the Man in suit to show its contents to the camera.

Youth program participants.description: Several individuals, including a man in a beige hoodie and a woman with braided hair, seated at desks working on computers in the office, surrounded by framed photos of young people and holiday decorations, representing the youth-focused environment; the voice of Elsie, a former participant, is heard recounting her personal struggles with drug dealing and arrests from a young age.

Woman in blue shirt.description: A woman with long dark hair, wearing a bright blue button-up shirt and a smartwatch with a red band on her left wrist, initially seen in a tense phone conversation where her serious, concerned voice discusses a release and follow-up status with an unseen male caller. She then speaks directly to the camera with expressive hand gestures, her voice becoming urgent and convicted as she explains how 'New York and Connecticut runs very different' and that 'this job has more to do with it,' emphasizing that 'we are fighting against a system that clearly is not fair for minorities' and that 'home is not safe for most of these kids who come here, unfortunately.'

Computer screen form.description: A close-up of a computer screen displaying a form with questions such as 'What is your desired salary? (enter a dollar amount)', indicating an application or intake process for a program or job opportunity.

Man in gray hoodie.description: A man wearing a gray hoodie, seated at the long table in the office, eating and drinking, appearing relaxed and focused on his meal, with earbuds in his ears.

Kitchen setting.description: A modern kitchen with stainless steel appliances, including a microwave and stove, open shelves stocked with food and household items, and a sink area, where the Woman in blue shirt continues her conversation with expressive gestures.

ThinkPad laptop.description: A black ThinkPad laptop with the logo visible on the lid, placed on the desk in front of the Woman in blue shirt as she works and speaks during her phone call and subsequent conversation.

raw_segments: The minute opens with a close-up of a young man with dark curly hair, wearing a dark puffer jacket over a black hoodie, gently cradling a calm baby in a pink hooded jacket on a wooden bench in a sunny suburban playground. A woman in a white coat sits beside him, conversing. Over this peaceful scene, a male voice states, "And I'm gonna do everything in my power to do that," followed by a female voice sharing a personal testimony, "I always believed in him... people used to tell me all the time, you know, he's bad for you, don't follow him... but I just closed my ears to it 'cause I believed in him. And to this day I believe in him." Subtle, reflective background music plays throughout. The scene then transitions to an urban street where the same man walks past a blue brick building labeled 'AVENUES FOR JUSTICE'. A narrator's voice identifies the organization, explaining, "Andrés Rodriguez also believes in John. The lawyer runs a non-profit called Avenues for Justice, which works to keep young people out of prison and provides young offenders an alternative to incarceration." The man enters the warmly lit office, decorated with holiday garlands and a small Christmas tree, where he interacts with Andrés Rodriguez, an older man in a black suit and mustache. The narrator continues, "Andrés is very troubled by the changes he's seen." Andrés Rodriguez then speaks earnestly to the camera, gesturing, reflecting on economic issues: "It's real easy to be homeless these days... because of the economic issue. I mean, look, this neighborhood. There was a time, even for me, where the landlord didn't give me heat. I look around the corner and get a bigger apartment for less money. That was the situation. I mean, it wasn't this hustle and this issue."

The video opens with the Man in suit, an older man with dark hair and a mustache, seated in a warmly lit Office interior decorated with holiday garlands, a small Christmas tree, and a bulletin board covered in papers. Dressed in a black suit and white shirt, he speaks directly to the camera with expressive hand gestures, holding a small white object. His earnest voice discusses housing statistics, noting that apartments he used to rent for $200 now cost $4000, emphasizing that "there is no housing" for those who want and need it. The camera zooms in on his face, capturing his concerned expression as he speaks. A brief exterior shot of a city street, likely in New York, shows traffic lights, parked cars, and a 'CAMPUS' sign, before returning to the Office interior. Here, the Man in suit stands and flips through a green folder, showing its printed materials and images to the camera, continuing to gesture as he speaks. A female narrator's voice then details how the COVID-19 pandemic worsened conditions, with a CUNY survey indicating 44% of New Yorkers struggled with rent, leading to numerous evictions. She further explains that job termination, domestic violence, mental illness, and addiction are other reasons people end up on the streets, mentioning "Ankhel" has been helping young people facing such challenges for 50 years. The Man in suit then returns to his seated position, his voice resuming to explain, "I always say that I'm a court advocacy program... I don't really deal in homelessness, however, this work takes me into..." The professional yet personal atmosphere of the office, with its water cooler, couch, and personal items on the desk, remains consistent throughout.

The video opens with a close-up of an older Man in suit, with dark hair and a mustache, wearing a black suit and white shirt, seated in a warmly lit Office interior decorated with holiday garlands and a small Christmas tree. He speaks expressively, holding a small white object and gesturing, discussing the issue of homelessness among young people, stating, "I'm representing a lot of young people... who in 2023 or since the pandemic, even before that, these kids are on their own, problems at home... problems that they create for themselves sometimes that create situations where they can't go home." The camera then pulls back to reveal more of the office, showing a water cooler, a couch, and a desk with a 'FACE MASK' box. Several individuals, including youth program participants, are seen working on computers at desks, with one typing on a laptop and another using a desktop with a Roland speaker. The walls are adorned with framed photos of young people, decorated with blue and gold tinsel, emphasizing the youth-focused program. A close-up shows a computer screen displaying a form with a field for 'What is your desired salary?'. The scene transitions to a framed photograph of a group of young people in a classroom setting. A Woman in blue shirt, with long dark hair and a smartwatch, then speaks in the same office environment, her voice narrating, "The center aims to offer young people a safe space. They can do their homework here, have lunch or learn how to improve their CV, for example. They also have access to counselors who can serve as positive role models." She continues, introducing a former participant, "One of them is Elsie, who first came to the non-profit many years ago to join its program." Elsie's voice then recounts her past, "At the age of 13, I started to sell drugs. Um, all the way up to about 16 and a half. And at 16, I was arrested three times." Behind the Woman in blue shirt, a window displays a golden wreath, with a printer and holiday decorations visible on a cabinet, maintaining the professional yet warm atmosphere focused on community engagement and youth development.

The video opens with a close-up of Elsie, a woman with long dark hair wearing a bright blue button-up shirt and a smartwatch with a red band, seated in an office. She speaks expressively, gesturing with her hands, reflecting on a pivotal time in her life: "at the same time I found out I was pregnant. So that just flipped my whole life around... to help me do that exactly, which is put me back in school, um, you know, get me a place. I was living with a mom at the time in a studio apartment... to prepare myself for being a young mother. Um, I was 16, I had no idea what I was getting myself into... between becoming a young mother and facing state prison time. Um, I think those are two barriers that I probably won't want to wish on anybody." As she speaks, the camera slowly zooms out to reveal her seated in front of a window adorned with a golden wreath, with a printer and holiday decorations visible on a cabinet behind her. The scene transitions to a wider shot of a communal office space where Elsie is now seated at a long table, working on a laptop. The room is decorated with a large 'Andrew Cuomo' banner, a calendar, and holiday garlands. A man in a gray hoodie is seen at the table, eating and drinking, while the sound of paper shuffling is heard. A narrator then states, "Now, Elsie is helping others. Today she's dealing with a client's case in Connecticut." Elsie then picks up her phone and begins a serious conversation, holding the phone to her ear and touching her face with an expression of concern or deep thought. The camera focuses closely on her face as she asks, "Hey, how are you? Um, to my understanding, he had a court appearance today, correct?" A male voice responds, "Yes, that's correct," before Elsie inquires, "And the outcome was?"

The video opens with a close-up of a Woman in a blue shirt, her expression tense and concerned as she engages in a serious phone conversation. Her voice, serious and questioning, asks, "to my understanding if he was being released, mom had to be there, correct?" An unseen male voice responds with a simple "No," to which she reiterates, "No?" She continues, explaining, "I want to see no follow up on the status because if we do need to go up there, we just need a heads up." She holds the phone to her ear, touching her face, indicating emotional strain, while a melancholic background music plays softly. The camera pulls back to reveal she is seated at a desk in a warmly lit office interior, working on a black ThinkPad laptop, with a water bottle and a colorful mural behind her. She concludes the call, saying, "Okay, appreciate you. All right. Thank you. Okay. Bye." She then looks down at her laptop before turning to speak directly to the camera with a serious demeanor, her hands gesturing expressively. Her voice becomes more urgent and convicted as she states, "Yep, they want to know nothing until tomorrow. That's another state. New York and Connecticut runs very different." The scene transitions to a modern kitchen setting, where she continues her impassioned explanation in front of stainless steel appliances and open shelves. Her gestures become more animated as she emphasizes her points, asserting, "You cannot possibly do this job if you just come in here just do a job and go home. This job has more to do with it. Mentally, we are fighting against a system that clearly is not fair for minorities." She concludes with conviction, "So it is like a balance that is just not fair, right? Home supposed to be the safe place. And safe home is not safe for most of these kids who come here, unfortunately."

event_blocks.summary: In a modern kitchen with stainless steel appliances, Elsie, a woman in a bright blue button-up shirt and a smartwatch, speaks expressively, gesturing with her hands about the need to "get it together" and how "most of our kids don't get a good deal" against a "system that the law comes in play." The scene shifts to an office interior, adorned with a 'Andrew Cuomo Youth Program' banner and holiday decorations, where Elsie is observed working on a black ThinkPad laptop while engaged in a phone conversation. A narrator explains that Elsie not only works at the non-profit but is also a student at NYU, noting her past journey was "anything but a given." Elsie then addresses the camera directly, hands clasped and with a serious expression, recounting personal experiences with trauma, the criminal justice system, homelessness, and domestic violence survival. She shares reflections directed at her younger self, stating "it's not my fault," "I am loved," and "there's people that do care for me," demonstrating a thoughtful and engaged demeanor.

event_blocks.summary: In an office adorned with holiday decorations, a woman in a blue shirt and smartwatch emphasizes the importance of confidence for children from shelters to foster a sense of worth. The scene shifts to an urban street with upbeat music, where a narrator introduces "LC" and mentions "John and Zoe" hoping to soon stand on their own. A woman in a beige coat exits a building and walks past a 'ONE WAY' sign, later joining a man in a puffer jacket at a Dunkin' Donuts counter to order. Transitioning to a sunny suburban street with brick houses, the man, now holding a baby in a pink hooded jacket, gently cradles and kisses the calm baby while walking, reflecting on the rapid passage of time and concurrent life events. He voices plans for an apartment with three cats, affirmed by the woman's "Mhm," and discusses his recent efforts in responsibility. The man, with dark curly hair, is then seen cradling and kissing the baby in the pink hooded jacket with red trim outdoors in the sunny suburban setting, expressing exhaustion from administrative tasks and paperwork, stating, "getting everything that we need together, you know, all the paperwork, all the information, having to renew stuff is, is just been a lot... I feel like I'm, I'm pooped. I'm ready to go to sleep and I wake up till next year." The baby, initially looking away, turns its curious eyes to the camera as passing cars are heard. The scene then shifts to a wider shot under an overpass, where the man and a woman in a white top and dark pants walk hand-in-hand toward a large white building, accompanied by melancholic orchestral music and on-screen credits for producers and DW News.

Baby in apartment.description: The baby, now in a pink hooded jacket with red trim, is being held by the man, appearing calm and possibly asleep, with its face serene and nestled against the man’s chest, serving as the affectionate focus of the man's speech about the passage of time and future family life.

Suburban street setting.description: A quiet, sunlit suburban street with brick houses, bare trees, a crosswalk, and a passing yellow school bus, creating a peaceful and family-friendly atmosphere.

raw_segments: The segment opens in an office where a woman in a bright blue button-up shirt and a smartwatch speaks expressively, her voice emphasizing the importance of confidence for children, particularly those who have been in shelters, to help them feel worthy. The office is decorated with holiday elements like a golden wreath and green garland. The scene then transitions to an outdoor urban setting, accompanied by upbeat, slightly dramatic background music and a female narrator's voice, which introduces "LC" as having come a long way, and "John and Zoe" as hoping to stand on their own two feet soon, remaining optimistic despite challenges. Visually, a woman with long dark hair in a beige coat exits a building and walks down a city sidewalk past a 'ONE WAY' sign. The narrative continues as the woman in the beige coat is then seen with a man in a dark puffer jacket at a Dunkin' Donuts counter, ordering. The scene shifts to a sunny suburban area with brick houses, where the man, now holding a baby in a pink hooded jacket, gently cradles and kisses the calm baby while walking. His voice is heard reflecting on how quickly time has flown since the baby was born, feeling like a month goes by in a week, and how everything is happening at the same time. He mentions future plans of living in an apartment with three cats, and the woman's voice briefly interjects with an "Mhm" in affirmation. He continues to speak about having done the most he's ever done in his life, particularly in trying to be responsible.

The video opens with a close-up of the Man with dark curly hair in a puffer jacket, gently cradling the Baby in a pink hooded jacket with red trim as he walks outdoors in a sunny suburban setting. He speaks softly in a male voice, expressing his exhaustion from administrative tasks, stating, "getting everything that we need together, you know, all the paperwork, all the information, having to renew stuff is, is just been a lot... I feel like I'm, I'm pooped. I'm ready to go to sleep and I wake up till next year." He occasionally kisses the baby's head, while the baby, initially looking away, turns its wide, curious eyes directly at the camera. The sounds of passing cars are audible in the background. The scene then transitions to a wider shot from under an overpass, where the man and a Woman in a white top and dark pants walk hand-in-hand away from the camera along a paved path toward a large white building, framed by a metal gate. Orchestral background music begins, adding a slightly melancholic tone. On-screen text credits Producer: Marc Erath and Executive Producer: Katharina Kroll, alongside DW News branding and social media handles. The bright, natural lighting maintains a calm, familial atmosphere.
